title: Fix a typo in batch_np_matrix_to_pycolmap
body:
The current implementation can cause `reproj_mask` to be undefined in `batch_np_matrix_to_pycolmap()` if `max_reproj_error = None` causing the following: 

```
if masks is not None and reproj_mask is not None:
UnboundLocalError: local variable 'reproj_mask' referenced before assignment
```

Initializing `reproj_mask` beforehand fix that error.

step_plan.summary: Initialize reproj_mask to None at the start of batch_np_matrix_to_pycolmap to avoid an UnboundLocalError when max_reproj_error is None, preserving existing mask-combining logic and preventing crashes.

steps.reason: Prevent UnboundLocalError when max_reproj_error is None: reproj_mask is only assigned inside the if block, but later code references it unconditionally. Initializing reproj_mask = None immediately before the `if max_reproj_error is not None:` preserves the current boolean checks (reproj_mask is not None) and avoids referencing an unbound local variable.
steps.side_effects: Low risk. No public API changes or behavioral changes except avoiding a crash when max_reproj_error is None. Downstream callers will see identical mask-combining behavior because reproj_mask remains None when not computed. Recommended to add unit tests exercising combinations of masks (None / not None) and max_reproj_error (None / float) to ensure no regressions. No changes to other functions are required, but tests or code that relied on the UnboundLocalError (unlikely) would be affected.
